transcript.title: Opening
transcript.text: Good morning. This is your Grace Bath World briefing for Wednesday, June 24. Over the next half hour: top headlines, bhopal and madhya pradesh, across india, around the world, business and markets, money, tax and personal finance, science and technology, development and policy. Let's begin.

transcript.title: Top headlines
transcript.text: First, the stories leading the news this morning. Delhi 10-yr-old rape and murder: How father's memory, digital trail led police to suspected cabbie. A father's fragmented memory of a white car and a yellow plate initiated a swift investigation. Investigators pieced together a digital trail, including CCTV footage and cab aggregator data, to track down Basu Kumar Singh, a driver accused of abducting, raping, and murdering a 10-year-old girl. The suspect was apprehended within seven hours of the initial report, later confessing to the heinous crime. Ronaldo ends 60-year wait, breaks Portugal World Cup record. Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice as Portugal thrashed Uzbekistan 5-0 to strengthen their FIFA World Cup 2026 knockout hopes and rewrite national history. The 41-year-old became Portugal's all-time leading World Cup goalscorer with 10 goals, surpassing Eusébio's 60-year-old record, while also extending his own record as the first player to score in six different World Cup tournaments. Oracle layoffs: Company confirms how many employees got 6AM email from leadership. Oracle has initiated significant layoffs globally, impacting thousands of employees across US, India, and other regions. These workforce reductions, attributed to management changes, AI adoption, and strategic shifts, resulted in a 13 percent drop in its workforce. The company incurred substantial restructuring costs, with severance packages reportedly less generous than industry peers. Major fire breaks out in Delhi labour settlement near Udyog Bhawan, no injuries reported. A fire erupted in labour slums near Delhi's Udyog Bhawan on Wednesday, originating from an electric panel and spreading to temporary structures. Around 20 fire tenders battled the blaze, bringing it under control by 5:30 am. No casualties have been reported. Indian student headed to US sent back from Amsterdam, unaware his F-1 visa had been revoked. An Indian student recently shared a harrowing experience on his way to the US after a vacation in the US. After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn, makes 540 workers millionaires. A small-town US family has sold their Louisiana-based electrical-equipment company Fibrebond to power-management giant Eaton for $1.7 billion, and made sure their 540 full-time workers got a $240 million cut of it. Former CEO Graham Walker wrote a single clause into the deal: 15 percent of proceeds for the staff, none of whom held any equity. The average bonus works out to around $443,000 per worker. After annus horribilis 2025, Indian DGCA to come under US FAA scanner this November. NEW DELHI: Come Nov and the Indian Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) will come under the US Federal Aviation Administration's (FAA) scanner. 'Next question': Ronaldo refuses journalist's question about Messi. Cristiano Ronaldo responded to criticism in emphatic fashion, scoring twice in Portugal's 5-0 World Cup victory over Uzbekistan to become the first player to score in six different World Cups and Portugal's all-time leading World Cup goalscorer. The veteran also dismissed retirement talk, declared "I'm back," and showed little interest in discussing Lionel Messi after the match. 'I will take the guarantee of Kohli, Rohit': Ex-India star makes bold 2027 ODI WC claim. Former India cricketer Mohammad Kaif has backed Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma to play in the 2027 ODI World Cup, citing Rohit's fitness, passion and improved batting approach. Kaif also believes India will be favourites for the tournament, with most members of the 2023 World Cup squad, including Bumrah, Gill, Rahul, Iyer and Kuldeep, expected to remain part of the team. After laying off hundreds of engineers, GM now replaces more than 1,000 workers with 50 robots. General Motors has introduced 50 collaborative robots at its Michigan Factory Zero, reportedly leading to over 1,000 job eliminations. This move, aimed at cost-cutting and efficiency, sparks criticism from the United Auto Workers union, which views it as a threat to jobs. GM claims the robots support human workers, but the UAW president calls it a "fight for humanity" amid broader automation concerns in the industry. Pune realtor murder: Fiancée, lover arrested for pushing him off cliff; CCTV unravel conspiracy. Pune: CCTV footage showing a man wearing a hoodie in peak summer and trailing a couple gave investigators the clue that untangled a 25-year-old Pune realtor's June 18 murder after his fiancée had everyone at home believe he "accidentally" fell to his death from Lohagad Fort while posing for a picture. Delhi Capitals cricketer accused of sexual relationship under false promise of marriage. Bengal and Delhi Capitals cricketer Abishek Porel faces serious allegations after a woman filed a police complaint accusing him of a broken promise of marriage, assault, and criminal intimidation. The woman claims a three-year relationship soured last year, with Porel allegedly backing out of marriage plans. Porel has vehemently denied the accusations, calling them baseless and suggesting they are timed with his recent cricketing success. Bank of India wins rupees 100 crore case against Nirav Modi. LONDON: A London high court judge ruled in Bank of India's favour on Tuesday, holding that fugitive diamond businessman Nirav Modi does owe the public sector bank over $10.7 million (a little over rupees 100 crore). FIFA WC: Ronaldo scripts history in Portugal's dominant 5-0 victory over Uzbekistan. Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice as Portugal crushed Uzbekistan 5-0 in Houston to strengthen their place in the FIFA World Cup 2026 knockout stages. The captain opened the scoring before adding a record-breaking second goal, becoming the first player to score in six consecutive World Cups and surpassing Eusébio as Portugal's all-time leading World Cup scorer. IAF tender seeking support package for all 36 Rafales, debunks Pakistan's claim. News News: NEW DELHI: A latest Indian Air Force (IAF) tender seeking 'bridge support' for all 36 Rafale fighter jets India bought from France has contradicted Pa. Taylor Swift is reportedly struggling with the Blake Lively fallout. Taylor Swift and Blake Lively's bond has only worsened over the last few months as rumors swirl about the actress being invited to the pop star's wedding to Travis Kelce which is set to happen in the next few weeks. While Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce focus on their wedding, it seems the pop star has gone through a wave of emotions. The Vikings and Lions are linked to a potential trade for Colts standout. The Indianapolis Colts' star player Anthony Richardson has been actively seeking a trade from the team since February 2026 which has raised eyebrows and sparked chaos about his future in the league. Amid this, the Detroit Lions and the Minnesota Vikings have emerged as potential landing spots for him ahead of the new season. A tale of two deals: Trump's 'infinity' inspections meets Iran's 'not so fast'. TOI correspondent from Washington: One side believes that it has come away with a deal. Flu kills 1.2 lakh/year in India, senior citizens bear the brunt. NEW DELHI: Influenza claims an estimated 1.2 lakh lives in India every year, with nearly two-thirds of the deaths occurring among people aged 65 years and above. Stampede-like situation at Ranchi stadium during Jharkhand T20 League final; several injured. NEW DELHI: A stampede-like situation was reported at the JSCA Stadium in Ranchi during the final of the Jharkhand T20 League after a large crowd gathered at the venue on Tuesday leaving several injured. Courts now face challenge of preserving public trust, says CJI. Chief Justice Surya Kant Tuesday acknowledged that the judiciary faced the challenge of preserving the public's faith in it and said the panacea lay in the judges' knowledge, integrity and commitment to expeditious and fair justice delivery. Fields of work by foreign-funded NGOs outlined, proselytisation excluded. NEW DELHI: Eliminating any room for ambiguity in activities permitted in the country with foreign contributions, the home ministry has notified a schedule of 105 purposes from which NGOs and associations, both ones seeking fresh registration and those already registered under the Foreign Contribution Regulation Act (FCRA), must choose while declaring their purpose or purposes of registration. No need to panic for England - but this was a reality check. England's drab goalless draw with Ghana is no cause to panic - but it does serve as a reality check, writes Phil McNulty.

Comprehensive up-to-date news coverage, aggregated from sources all over the world by Google News. Mumbai rain fury: Season's heaviest overnight spell brings over 200 mm rain to several areas. Mumbai experienced a torrential overnight downpour, with several areas receiving over 200 mm of rain in just eight hours. Malwani, Malad, topped the charts with 271 mm. The intense rainfall caused waterlogging in low-lying areas, including Hindmata and Andheri Subway, but civic teams worked swiftly to clear the accumulation, largely restoring traffic flow by morning.

transcript.title: Across India
transcript.text: Turning to the big national stories. Cabinet reshuffle buzz as minister Kurian resigns. NEW DELHI: George Kurian's resignation as minister of state for minority affairs and PM Modi calling on the President re-ignited the buzz about the likelihood of a ministerial reshuffle before monsoon session of Parliament, slated for the second half of July. Cabbie abducts, rapes and kills 10-year-old in Delhi; shot by cops during escape bid. NEW DELHI: A 10-year-old girl was allegedly snatched from her family that was sleeping on a pavement in south Delhi, sexually assaulted and murdered by a cab driver early Monday morning, reports Abhay. Ladakh bandh to protest 'breach' of agreement in Delhi talks. SRINAGAR: A month after talks between Ladakh groups and a Union home ministry (MHA) panel in Delhi, the Union Territory observed Tuesday a complete shutdown against the Centre's alleged backtracking on understandings reached during the parleys. Poland donor's stem cells save Kashmir boy suffering from rare immune condition. SRINAGAR: The stem cell donor was from Poland. Kolkata Port, Adani Ports sign MoU to restore iconic Kumartuli Ghat ahead of Durga Puja. NEW DELHI: Syama Prasad Mookerjee Port Kolkata (SMPK) and Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Limited (APSEZ) have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to restore and redevelop the historic Kumartuli Ghat riverfront in Kolkata, with work on the rupees 10-crore project set to be completed before this year's Durga Puja. 315 districts mapped for contingency measures to rescue farming operation as rain deficit rises to 43 percent. 111 districts identified as the most vulnerable due to low irrigation footprints. rupees 89,000-crore import bill sparks fresh push for made-in-India medical devices. NEW DELHI: From MRI machines and CT scanners to surgical implants and critical care equipment, India's dependence on imported medical devices is continuing to grow despite years of efforts to boost local manufacturing. 'Students' blood on his hands': CJP chief Dipke hits back at Pradhan during Jantar Mantar protest. NEW DELHI: Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) founder Abhijeet Dipke on Tuesday launched a sharp attack on Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan during an ongoing protest at Delhi's Jantar Mantar, accusing him of being responsible for student deaths linked to alleged examination irregularities. NHAI signs MoU with NCAER for research centre on economics of transportation, mobility and logistics. NEW DELHI: National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) on Tuesday signed a memorandum of understanding with the National Council of Applied Economic Research (NCAER) to establish a research centre on transportation, mobility and logistics. Qatar Emir dials PM Modi after Ras Laffan blast kills 12 Indians. NEW DELHi: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Tuesday spoke with Qatar's Emir, Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, following the explosion at the Ras Laffan Industrial City gas facility that killed 12 Indian nationals and left dozens injured. 'Will face same fate as TMC and Shiv Sena': BJP claims several BJD leaders 'keen' to jump ship in Odisha. NEW DELHI: The ruling BJP in Odisha on Tuesday claimed that several opposition leaders are keen to join the saffron camp, drawing sharp criticism from ex-CM Naveen Patnaik's Biju Janata Dal (BJD), which called it an attempt to "divert" attention from law and order issues in the state. Voter ID row: Third non-bailable warrant issued against actor Prakash Raj. NEW DELHI: A third non-bailable warrant was issued against Actor Prakash Raj after he failed to appear before a Bengaluru court in a case alleging that his name figured in four electoral rolls spread across three states. Cancelled Bali trip and a missing passport: Inside a fiancée and her lovers' plan to kill Pune man. NEW DELHI: A cancelled Bali holiday, a mysteriously missing passport and a trek that ended in death have emerged as key elements in the police investigation into the alleged murder of 26-year-old Pune resident Ketan Agarwal. Delhi CM orders graft probe against two senior health officials over medicine procurement. Chief minister Rekha Gupta has ordered a corruption probe against two senior Delhi Health Department officers over alleged irregularities in the procurement of medicines and other medical supplies for government hospitals, officials said on Tuesday. Weather LIVE Updates: Rain Brings Relief From Heat In Delhi, More Showers Predicted. It is fuel made entirely from ethanol. Geography's Veto: The Limits of China's Economic Reach Into Nepal. China has plenty of promises and surveys, but all-year, all-weather throughput is not there. India provides Nepal contiguity, lowland railroad, and dozens of crossings. 5 Arrested For Chicken-And-Beer Party On Boat In Varanasi's Ganga. transcript.title: Around the world
transcript.text: And here is what is happening around the world. UN says it will evacuate sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz, as Rubio warns against tolls. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned Iran that no country can charge fees for ships to travel through the strait. Israeli troops kill two in south Lebanon after lull in fighting, authorities say. Israel's military says the two men were Hezbollah operatives, but the Iran-backed group accuses it of a ceasefire violation. Germany rail network briefly halted nationwide due to IT malfunction. Rail company Deutsche Bahn had to pause train services across the country for more than two-and-a-half hours. Dozens of ships head through Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran deal. Traffic in the waterway has risen since the US and Iran signed a deal aimed at ending the war, including 42 ships on Saturday alone. UN commission of inquiry says Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children. Israel rejects the new report by the three-member expert panel, calling it a "libellous sham". Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba sues US government over defence blacklist. It is suing the US defence department after it was added to a list of firms with ties to the Chinese military. Zambia ex-president's family wins latest legal battle over what should happen to his body. Edgar Lungu's family and the Zambian government remained in dispute 12 months after he died. Spider which uses spring trap to capture prey discovered in Australia. Researchers say their hunting method - which allows them to prey on dangerous ants - is unprecedented. US top court says Rastafarian man cannot sue prison guards who cut his dreadlocks. The former Louisiana inmate argued his Rastafarian faith was violated after prison officials focibly shaved off his hair. Irish government to contribute £197m to cross-border rail services. The money will come from the last allocation from the Irish government's Shared Island Fund. Trump anticipates better relationship with Colombia under new leader. Abelardo de la Espriella, who preliminary results suggest is Colombia's next president, had Trump's endorsement. Montreal shooting leaves officer, civilian and suspect dead. It was the first time in 24 years that a Montreal police officer was killed in the line of duty, the city's police chief said. Ransom note claims Nancy Guthrie died after abduction. On Tuesday, Today show presenter Savannah Guthrie begged anyone with information to come forward, saying, "We are in agony." Iran says no new commitments on nuclear sites after Vance says inspectors to be invited back. Iran's foreign ministry says it made "no new commitments" on nuclear inspections after talks in Switzerland. Dettol apologises after ad to clean up 'toxic men' backfires in China. The ad sparked accusations of sexism in China, after featuring a man looking for a partner who is "not tainted by other men". Inquiry ordered after building fire kills 15 in north India city. Witnesses described people jumping from windows to escape, while rescue teams broke through a wall to save lives. Myanmar army killed over 700 civilians in six months, UN says. The new UN report says the 702 civilian deaths over six months last year included 153 children. Russian troop build-up threatens city seen as key to seizing Ukraine's Donbas. If Kostyantynivka falls, Russian forces would be able push towards Ukraine's last remaining strongholds in the east. Tesla crash that killed a woman under US federal investigation. A Tesla driver on Friday said he was using 'self-driving' technology after his vehicle sped into a Texas home. At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion. The city's main liquified natural gas (LNG) processing site suffered a "technical accident" in the Ras Laffan industrial zone. Syrians reject Trump's call for Syria to combat Hezbollah in Lebanon. Residents in Damascus rejected US President Donald Trump's suggestion that Syria should confront Hezbollah in Lebanon. Kenyan minister orders halt to construction of US Ebola facility. Decision comes after Aden Duale was held in contempt for ignoring previous high court ruling to stop work Kenya's health minister told a court he had ordered preparations for a US-run Ebola quarantine facility to stop, after being held in contempt for ignoring a previous order to end work. Many Kenyans strongly oppose the facility , with deadly protests erupting since the complex was announced in May for US citizens evacuated from the Democratic Republic of Congo, which is grappling with a widespread Ebola outbreak . Continue reading... Budimir gives Croatia a 1-0 win to eliminate Panama from the World Cup. Croatia are third in Group L, with England and Ghana on four points each, heading into the final round of matches. UK prioritised ties with UAE over averting mass atrocities in Sudan, MPs to be told. Foreign Office failed to act on warnings of genocide due to 'pressure' from emirates, Yale human rights investigator will tell a parliamentary select committee The British government had received intelligence that Ethiopia appeared to be supporting a genocidal militia in Sudan's civil war as far back as 2024 but did not go public with the news for fear of upsetting the United Arab Emirates (UAE), a parliamentary committee will hear. In May 2024, officials from the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) told Nathaniel Raymond , an American human rights investigator at Yale University, that "significant private pressure" from the UAE meant the UK would not publicly divulge information linking Ethiopia and the emirates to their support for the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces. Continue reading...

transcript.title: Business and markets
transcript.text: Moving to business, the economy and the markets. India tells Pakistan to look in its own mirror. India on Tuesday rejected Pakistan Defence Minister Khawaja Asifs remarks on Jammu and Kashmir, calling them an attempt to divert attention from Islamabads internal issues. US touts Iran deal as farm windfall; Tehran denies. A tentative US-Iran deal to end hostilities promises a financial boost for American farmers, with President Trump asserting frozen Iranian assets will fund US crop purchases. SpaceX bond debut creates a $89 bn cosmic demand. SpaceX is witnessing immense investor interest, with demand reaching approximately $89 billion for its inaugural US bond sale. UN starts evacuation of 11,000 seafarers in Hormuz. A major evacuation plan is now underway for over 11,000 seafarers aboard hundreds of ships stranded in the Gulf. India-UK FTA gives birth to new 'law' of attraction. Indian law firms are actively seeking UK partnerships ahead of a free trade agreement, anticipating a surge in cross-border legal work. Post BrahMos, has India found its next bestseller?. Indias defence export story, long centred on the BrahMos supersonic missile, may be expanding to include a new system gaining global attention. WhatsApp's next big idea may come from India. Meta's $900 million investment in Cred and the reported appointment of founder Kunal Shah to a global role at WhatsApp marks a significant shift in how the messaging platform may evolve. Rubio arrives in UAE to head off Gulf Arab unease. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is in the UAE to address concerns over a new Iran deal. US Senate joins House in voting to halt Iran war. In a significant move, the U.S. India to operationalise up to 60 mines in FY27. India is set to launch 50 to 60 new mines this fiscal year, building on the 36 operationalized in the previous year. India-US push trade pact as tariff clock ticks. India and the US are pushing for an interim trade pact, with high-level talks led by Piyush Goyal and Jamieson Greer aiming for a deal before July 24. India to resume train coach exports to Bangladesh. India is set to export a train rake of up to 20 coaches to Bangladesh next month, marking a significant resumption of trade. MoS Kurian's exit fuels Cabinet rejig buzz. Union Minister of State for Minority Affairs George Kurian has resigned, sparking speculation of a Cabinet reshuffle. India 13th in QS Future Skills Index 2027. India has built formidable scale over the past decade, supported by rapid economic growth, a large pool of graduates and the world's largest IT workforce, according to global higher education expert Quacquarelli Symonds. SC halts early release of Bowbazar blast convict. The Supreme Court has halted the Delhi High Court's decision to grant premature release to Md Rashid Khan, a life convict in the 1993 Bowbazar blast. Oman assures toll-free movement via Hormuz. Oman has pledged toll-free passage through the Strait of Hormuz, a move welcomed by India. SC asks Calcutta HC to hear SIR-linked ration plea. The Supreme Court has directed a petitioner to take his plea challenging the exclusion of individuals from West Bengal's ration system to the Calcutta High Court. A test could make or break Trump's H-1B $100K fee. A US court blocked a $100,000 H-1B visa fee, deeming it an unconstitutional tax imposed by presidential authority without congressional approval. Gujarat sends classrooms on wheels to the Rann. Gujarat has launched 28 'Rannshala' buses, transforming them into mobile classrooms for children of salt pan workers in the Little Rann of Kutch. Trump to present FIFA World Cup trophy. FIFA President Gianni Infantino revealed plans for President Donald Trump to join him in presenting the World Cup Final trophy on July 19 in East Rutherford, New Jersey. Brent it like Delhi? India shines while others struggle. Shipping through the crucial Strait of Hormuz is showing signs of recovery, with daily transits increasing significantly, though still far below pre-war levels. US visa denied: "Did you watch yesterday's match?". A viral Reddit post detailing US visa interview experiences at the Delhi Consulate reveals that weak or unconvincing answers, not just documentation, can lead to rejections.

transcript.title: Science and technology
transcript.text: Next, science and technology. The US is leading the new space race. The US is leading the new space race – but other countries are close behind The Conversation India's experience in diabetes care could guide other developing countries: V. Mohan. In an article in Diabetologia, the Chennai-based specialist argues for local solutions and integrated care; recommends tech interventions such as an AI chatbot, telemedicine, and a physician decision support tool Reconnect public health with people's needs. Access to curative care and stronger institutions must anchor India's health policy

transcript.title: Sign-off
transcript.text: That is your briefing. Stay sharp, and have a strong day.
